title: Post-Training Quantization
learning_outcomes: Explain GPTQ second-order Taylor expansion math.

content_sections.title: Quantization Error Minimization and GPTQ
content_sections.content:
Post-Training Quantization (PTQ) compresses model parameters without requiring retraining. **GPTQ** models quantization as a quadratic error minimization problem. It uses the inverse Hessian matrix $\mathbf{H}^{-1}$ to adjust remaining weights to compensate for quantization errors:

$$\Delta \mathbf{w} = -\frac{w_i - \text{quant}(w_i)}{[\mathbf{H}^{-1}]_{ii}} \mathbf{H}^{-1}_{:, i}$$

This updates weight matrices column by column, preserving model capacity even at 4-bit configurations.

content_sections.title: Activation-aware Weight Quantization (AWQ)
content_sections.content: **AWQ (Activation-aware Weight Quantization)** protects the top 1% of salient channels (which carry the most information during inference) from heavy quantization. Instead of quantizing them directly, AWQ scales these channels up before quantization, protecting them from rounding errors and preserving model performance.
